risks: No managed identity configuration for ADF or Databricks — credentials may be managed insecurely
Storage account uses Standard_LRS with no geo-redundancy — risk of data loss in a regional outage
No Azure Key Vault resource for managing secrets such as sqlAdministratorLogin password
No network isolation (VNet, private endpoints) for any resource — all services exposed to public internet
No monitoring or diagnostics configuration (Log Analytics, diagnostic settings) across any resource
Databricks standard SKU limits VNet injection and advanced security features (RBAC, audit logging)
No resource tagging strategy for cost management and operational governance
correctionsRecommended: Add managed identity configuration to ADF and Databricks for credential-free authentication to storage and Synapse
Upgrade storage SKU from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS or Standard_GRS for production reliability
Add an Azure Key Vault resource to securely store SQL admin passwords and connection strings
Add a VNet resource with private endpoints for Synapse, Storage, and Databricks to enforce network isolation
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on all resources for observability
Consider upgrading Databricks to Premium SKU to enable VNet injection and enhanced security features
Add resource tags (environment, project, cost-center) to all resources for operational governance
Make ADF runtime dependency on the storage account explicit in its dependencies array
